Plaintiff Drumm Investors, Llc'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach (Continued From 8/29/11)
Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF DRUMM INVESTORS, LLC'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT.

APPEARANCES: Lily A. North, Esq. 262-4528 & H. Joseph Escher III, Esq. of Dechert LLP for pltf & moving party; Beth H. Parker, Esq. 356-3051 & Maulik G. Shah, Esq. 356-3022 of Arnold & Porter LLP for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Court adopts its tentative ruling that the request for writ of attachment is granted. The defendant's opposition was untimely under 484.060(a) and case law requires strict construction of attachment statutes. Jordan-Lyon Prod., Ltd. v. Cineplex Odeon Corp., (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1459, 1466. However, even assuming that CCP section 475 allowed this Court to disregard this error, the Court finds that plaintiff has established a likelihood of prevailing on the merits of the complaint and defendant is unlikely to prevail on the merits in the cross-complaint. Accordingly, the writ of attachment is for the amount of $4.4 million. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
